Job Title: Senior Finance Systems Data Consultant - JDE & Azure
Ad Text:
The Role:
  
My client is a reputable Australian organisation on the lookout for a Senior Finance Systems Data Consultant to join a large-scale finance systems transformation for a major organisation based in Sydney CBD. This role is 5 days in their modern offices, including a brand-new gym, saunas + more.
  
This is a 12-month fixed-term contract with extensions highly likely and a genuine pathway to a permanent role for the right person. You'll be embedded in a complex, high-stakes program and play a key role in shaping and delivering data outcomes across a significant finance systems overhaul. If you thrive in transformation environments and know your way around finance systems, this one's for you.
  
Key Responsibilities:
  
• Partner with finance, transformation and technology stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions across a large-scale finance systems transformation
• Work with JD Edwards (JDE) data structures and support migration, integration, or reporting activities related to the platform
• Design and implement automation patterns and reusable components e.g. orchestration, error handling, reconciliation and controls.
• Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines, integrations, and data models to support business and reporting needs
• Partner with Finance to delivery identified automation initiatives across end-to-end processes (Record-to-report, Procure-to-pay, Order-to-Cash etc)
• Leverage Azure data services to build and optimise scalable data solutions
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams including finance, IT, and program delivery
• Ensure data quality, integrity, and governance standards are met throughout the program
• Translate complex data and technical concepts clearly for non-technical stakeholders
• Contribute to documentation, design artefacts, and data architecture decisions
  
About You:
  
• Extensive experience working with Finance Systems and finance modules
• Strong SQL, Data migration, ETL and Integration experience, with Microsoft stack a requirement.
• Demonstrated experience working on finance systems transformations - this is essential
• Hands-on experience with JD Edwards (JDE) is highly regarded
• Comfortable navigating large, complex program environments with multiple stakeholders
• Strong communicator - able to engage confidently with both technical and business audiences

Norton Rose Fulbright provides a full scope of legal services to the world's preeminent corporations and financial institutions. The global law firm has more than 3,000 lawyers advising clients across more than 50 locations worldwide covering Europe, the United States, Canada, Latin America, Asia, Australia, Africa and the Middle East.

We are seeking a highly capable Project & Change Manager to lead and deliver strategic transformation relating to the integration of our EMEA and Australian Finance Functions. This hybrid role combines 75% project management responsibilities with 25% change management & adoption, ensuring that projects are delivered effectively while new ways of working are successfully embedded. Based in Sydney, this role operates within a complex, global environment and will work across multi-disciplinary teams to drive business value, optimise processes, and ensure change is understood, adopted, and sustained.

Key Responsibilities

Project Management (75%)

Lead the end-to-end delivery of medium-to-large scale transformation projects, including business case development, requirements gathering, process improvement and implementation.
Act as the key contact between Sydney and London which may involve alternative time zone working.
Develop and maintain project documentation: project plans, RAID logs, action logs, risk assessments, status reporting, benefits tracking.
Analyse business processes and systems to identify opportunities for efficiency, standardisation, and optimisation.
Provide structure and guidance on ways of working, organisational design, and operational improvements.
Facilitate requirements sessions, and cross-functional discussions.
Identify and manage interdependencies across projects and business units.
Track and report project performance, KPIs, and benefits realisation.
Support colleagues by educating them on project delivery tools and frameworks.
Act decisively, operate with pace, and navigate ambiguity in a changeable environment.

Change Management & Adoption (25%)

Design and implement change and adoption approaches that align to programme needs and ensure successful adoption of new processes, tools, or technology.
Conduct stakeholder mapping, change impact assessments, and organisational readiness analyses.
Develop integrated communication and engagement plans using a range of media including written, graphic, digital, and face-to-face channels.
Develop and deliver training strategies to drive awareness, understanding, and adoption.
Define and track change success metrics, business value, ROI, and benefits realisation.
Partner with senior stakeholders to understand needs, expectations, and alignment to broader strategy.
Support horizon scanning, market insights, and analysis of impacts to the business model and workforce.
Contribute to the prioritisation of transformation opportunities across business functions.

Key Skills & Experience

Essential

Proven experience delivering complex, multi-geography transformation projects, ideally within professional services or similar industries.
Strong understanding of project management methodologies (e.g., PRINCE2, Agile, Waterfall, hybrid approaches).
Demonstrated experience in change management principles, frameworks, and communication strategies.
Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills, including the ability to deliver clear, persuasive presentations.
Strong analytical and problemâ€‘solving skills, including process analysis and modelling.
Experience managing senior stakeholders and influencing at all levels of an organisation.
High attention to detail, strong organisational skills, and the ability to prioritise within a fast-paced environment.
Flexible, adaptable, and comfortable working in ambiguous contexts.
Highly motivated, collaborative, and pragmatic self-starter.

Desirable

Understanding of digital communications and innovative engagement methods.
Exposure to organisational design activities.
Professional qualifications such as PRINCE2, APMP, Scrum, or Six Sigma.

Who is Plenti? 

Plenti is a fintech lender, providing faster, fairer loans by leveraging its smart technology. Plenti is a dynamic and innovative business that is growing strongly. By continuing to deliver better customer experiences, Plenti is taking market share from incumbent players in the personal lending, renewable energy, and automotive finance markets.

We are a fast moving and ambitious business that seeks to recruit smart and capable people, who can take ownership of their role to help the business thrive. With over 250 people based in Australia, Plenti is of a size where everyone can make a difference in their role and help us realise our very big ambitions as a team, as we go about building Australia’s best lender.

Plenti is a founder led business that launched in 2014, listed on the ASX since 2020 with annual revenue of over $250 million and a loan portfolio of over $3 billion.

About the role:
The Head of Data will lead Plenti’s data, analytics and business intelligence capability, redefining how data is generated, governed and leveraged across the organisation. This is a pivotal leadership role responsible for scaling our modern Databricks-based platform, building a best-in-class BI function, and driving the shift from retrospective reporting to forward-looking, automated decisioning embedded within customer and operational journeys.

Partnering closely with Risk, Finance, Product, Marketing, Sales and Operations, the Head of Data will shape the strategy, operating model and culture needed to position data as a core enabler of growth, risk management and operational performance.

Success in this role will require strong commercial acumen, a proven track record of building and leading high-performing data teams, and the ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights that drive business decisions.

Key responsibilities:
Data Strategy & Execution

Define and execute a clear data strategy aligned to Plenti’s growth, risk and efficiency objectives

Map how data is generated and used across the business and identify opportunities to unlock incremental value

Translate strategic priorities into a sequenced roadmap with clear ownership, milestones and outcomes

Balance rapid delivery of high-impact use cases with long-term platform and capability build

Business Intelligence & Insights

Establish a best-in-class BI capability as the single source of truth for business performance, defining and governing core metrics, semantic layers and reporting standards

Replace manual and fragmented reporting with scalable, automated BI solutions accessible to executives, teams and partners

Lead the evolution from descriptive reporting (“what happened”) to diagnostic, predictive and prescriptive insights

Embed actionable insights into workflows through dashboards, alerts and tools that empower business users to act

Platform, Architecture and Governance

Own the data platform — the design and evolution of our Databricks-based environment, ensuring scalable, reliable and cost-effective pipelines and architecture

Productionise models in partnership with Engineering, Risk and Product, embedding them into customer and operational workflows

Implement robust governance across data quality, lineage, privacy and regulatory compliance

Leadership and Operating Model

Lead a high-performing team across data engineering, BI and analytics

Define the operating model that maximises impact and alignment across data disciplines

Foster a data-driven culture built on continuous improvement and shared accountability

Stakeholder Engagement

Advocate for data across the business, partnering with executives to identify high-value use cases and drive adoption

Report transparently on data platform performance, usage, ROI and risk

Establish cross-functional forums to align on priorities, resolve issues and drive execution


About you:
You are a senior data leader who operates at the intersection of strategy, technology, and business. You combine strong technical depth with commercial acumen and are known for leading transformational change—shifting organisations from fragmented reporting toward cohesive, insight-driven decision-making. 

You bring a balance of hands-on understanding and strategic leadership, with the ability to engage deeply on platform, architecture, and analytics while influencing at executive level.

You will have: 

10+ years' of experience, with 5 years in leadership across large-scale data and analytics teams in complex, regulated organisations

Proven experience transitioning organisations from reporting-led models to intelligence-led, insight-driven environments

Strong track record of building, leading, and scaling high-performing, multidisciplinary data teams

Demonstrated success in delivering data strategies that translate into measurable business outcomes

Deep expertise across modern cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks), data engineering, BI, and advanced analytics / AI/ML

Strong commercial acumen with the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders

Experience in financial services or lending is highly regarded; experience operating in a regulated environment is essential


What's life like at Plenti? 

Working at Plenti means joining a team that is high-performing, diverse, customer-focused and collaborative. It’s a place where everyone can “make it happen” and share in the results and success. If being in a dynamic industry as part of a market-leading fintech sounds like you, then join to help us reshape Australia’s financial eco-system for the good.

In return, you will enjoy a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship to take your career further. We offer flexible working and volunteer and study leave to develop your skills. We also have monthly social events, office snacks, company Townhalls and D&I initiatives and events to help you connect with your colleagues. 

Plenti is committed to a diverse and inclusive workforce. 

We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and people with disabilities. When you apply, let us know of any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process so we can accommodate you.

Randstad is searching for a skilled and accomplished Annual Report Coordinator who possesses a keen eye for detail and excels in their field. This role requires the ability to build and sustain strong relationships. Our collaborative department is in need of an enthusiastic individual who embraces challenges and is committed to making a positive impact within reporting for the state government sector.

About your new role

The Annual Report Coordinator will be responsible for the end-to-end production of an organisation's most significant yearly publication. In this role, you act as a strategic liaison between diverse departments such as Finance, Legal, Communications and Sustainability to gather complex data and transform it into a cohesive narrative that reflects the company's performance and vision. You will establish and enforce a rigorous production timeline, ensuring that all contributors meet their deadlines so the report can be tabled or published in compliance with regulatory requirements. Beyond administrative oversight, you provide critical quality control by fact-checking figures, managing version history during intensive editing cycles and collaborating with designers to ensure the document is both visually engaging and accessible to stakeholders

What you will need to exceed

Deep understanding of the overarching legal requirements for public bodies.
Mastery of the 2018 Standing Directions and specific Financial Reporting Directions (FRDs) issued by the Assistant Treasurer.
Experience drafting the non-financial narrative of a report (performance highlights, chair/CEO reports, and strategic objectives).
Proven ability to manage a 6-month production cycle from initial planning to print/web publication.
Experience navigating complex governance structures, including Audit & Risk Committees and Board approvals.
The ability to translate complex financial data into "Plain English" for the general public.
An almost "obsessive" eye for detail to spot inconsistencies between the financial tables and the narrative text.
Previous experience in a Department or Agency is highly preferred.
A background in accounting helps significantly when reviewing financial statements.

Desirable

Experience in working for government

Next steps

If you are a reporting expert with a commitment to inclusivity, collaboration, and transformative change, we invite you to apply and be part of this inspiring journey.

At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

A major Australian Government agency is seeking a Lead Cybersecurity Analyst to strengthen its Cybersecurity function and uplift its governance, risk, and assurance capability.

If you’re a seasoned GRC professional who enjoys shaping policy, advising on risk, and embedding best-practice security across an organisation, this is a high-impact contract worth your attention.

Evaluate compliance against PSPF, ISM, and ACSC Essential Eight frameworks.


Provide expert advice on security controls, policies, and procedures.

Analyse changes in legislation/regulation and advise on organisational impacts.


Develop, uplift, and maintain Information Security governance processes.

Strong background in Cyber Governance, Risk & Compliance.

Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement, and documentation skills.


Highly Desirable
CISSP, CISM, or equivalent certifications.


Experience with IRAP, Essential Eight assessments, or security auditing.


Prior government experience and familiarity with APS Code of Conduct expectations.


You’ll be joining a federal agency with a clear mandate to protect Australian workplaces and ensure compliance with national standards. The Cyber team plays a critical role in safeguarding systems, data, and operations, and your expertise will directly influence the agency’s security posture and strategic uplift.

Job Title: Senior Quantitative Analyst: Credit Model Risk
Ad Text:
Senior Quantitative Analyst


Leading Bank
Permanent Role
Model Risk Team



The Company


Major player in the Australian banking sector, driven by a mission to be the most trusted financial partner for their client base. They prioritize a premier employee experience, fostering a culture where professional development and personal well-being go hand-in-hand.


The Role



In this position, you will act as a critical "check and balance" for the bank's lending models. You will transition away from model building to focus on high-level independent scrutiny and governance.



Model Deconstruction: You will rigorously audit the logic, data sources, and mathematical foundations of PD, LGD, and EAD models.

Regulatory Compliance: Your work ensures that the bank's internal systems align with strict APRA, Basel, and IFRS 9 requirements.

Advisory & Reporting: You will translate intricate technical findings into persuasive reports for executive leadership.

Innovation: Beyond standard audits, you will help modernize the bank's internal modelling toolkits and uplift validation standards.

Your Experience



Sector Background: You have a solid history in credit risk, specifically within provisioning, scorecards, or IRB frameworks.

Quantitative Mastery: You possess a sophisticated understanding of statistics and have a track record of applying these skills to real-world financial problems.

Technical Toolkit: You are highly proficient in coding with Python, R, SAS, or SQL.

Communication Skills: You can navigate high-level corporate environments, articulating complex "quant" concepts to non-technical stakeholders with ease.

Modern Workflow: While not mandatory, experience with Git or Jupyter environments will give your application a distinct edge.

Company description:



At INPEX, our energy is helping to shape a brighter future.

We are proudly supporting positive outcomes in Australian communities, through jobs, training, and social and economic benefits. Our Australian energy portfolio includes Ichthys LNG which we operate, plus participating interests in Prelude FLNG, Darwin LNG, Van Gogh and Ravensworth. We are actively pursuing carbon, capture, and storage opportunities to help provide a cleaner upstream business for Ichthys LNG.

Our inclusive culture is key to our success - valuing diversity and bringing us together as a connected and innovative team. As an international company, INPEX offers unique opportunities to collaborate with colleagues from across the globe. We are keen to attract and develop passionate, motivated, and safety-minded people from all backgrounds who want to help support society's growing need for secure, affordable, and clean energy.



Job description:



We are looking for an Analytics & Data Lead professional to provide strategic, technical and people leadership across analytics and data platforms, enabling high‑quality reporting, advanced analytics and data governance across INPEX Australia.

About the Role:

This is a senior, influential role within our Technology function. You will set direction for enterprise analytics and data platforms, partner closely with business leaders, and lead teams to deliver trusted, scalable and future‑ready analytics solutions that improve decision‑making, performance and efficiency.

Key responsibilities:

Strategy & Direction: Set and lead the strategy, roadmap and delivery of enterprise analytics, reporting, and data platforms aligned to business priorities.
People Leadership: Lead and develop onshore and offshore analytics and data teams, building capability, performance, engagement and a strong safety and inclusion culture.
Business Partnership: Partner with business leaders to understand information needs and translate them into high‑value analytics, reporting and data science solutions.
Platform & Data Governance: Embed analytics and data governance frameworks to ensure data quality, integrity, security, compliance and appropriate risk management.
Commercial Management: Manage budgets, planning, vendors and service providers to ensure cost‑effective, timely and high‑quality delivery of analytics and data platform initiatives.
Technical Leadership: Act as a recognised technical authority, applying emerging technologies and best practice to improve insight, scalability and business performance.

What we're looking for:

We welcome candidates from diverse backgrounds and experiences. You will be a strong fit for this role if you bring:

A tertiary qualification in Information Systems, Information Management, Data, Technology or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Demonstrated experience leading enterprise analytics and data platforms, including reporting, advanced analytics and data governance.
Strong ability to translate complex business requirements into practical, scalable analytics and data solutions.
Hands‑on experience with modern analytics platforms and tools (e.g. Microsoft Fabric, Power BI, SAP analytics technologies, or equivalent).
Proven capability to collaborate, influence and build trusted relationships with both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
A leadership approach that develops people, values inclusion and encourages diverse perspectives.

This role is for applicants residing in or willing to relocate to Perth.

Closing date for applications is 22 May 2026. INPEX reserves the right to close applications early should a suitable pool of candidates be identified. Please note that direct feedback on applications will only be provided to interviewed candidates.

We value diversity in our workplace - including beliefs, cultures, and lifestyles, so we can bring our true selves to work every day. If you believe that you have most but not all the requirements outlined above, we encourage you to still apply.

To support an inclusive recruitment experience for everyone, we invite candidates to advise us of any needs or adjustments that would be helpful throughout the process.



We offer:



Leave & Flexibility:

16 weeks of fully paid parental leave.
Options to purchase additional leave.
Dedication to flexible work arrangements, encompassing variable hours and remove work options
INPEX Foundation Day, discretionary leave, and community volunteering leave.

Remuneration & Financial Benefits:

Competitive remuneration package with performance‑based incentives.
Superannuation above the standard guarantee.
Salary packaging options, including novated leasing.
Reward and recognition programs, including access to discounts from over 350 retailers.

Career Growth & Development:

Clear pathways for career development and internal progression.
Structured leadership development programs with ongoing support.
Dedicated study leave and access to learning and development opportunities.

Wellbeing:

Access to wellbeing initiatives, including gym membership options and health resources.
A supportive workplace focused on safety, balance and sustainable work practices.

Additional benefits such as Japanese language classes are available at select locations, including Perth.

At GHD, we don’t just believe in the power of commitment, we live and breathe it every day.

That’s why we pledge to empower all of our people to make a positive impact.  Combining our deep technical expertise with the capabilities of our clients and partners, we respond to some of the most complex challenges facing our planet today.

Who are we looking for? 

We’re seeking an experienced Spatial Analyst to join our South-East Queensland Location Intelligence team. This role suits someone who combines strong technical capability with the confidence to engage meaningfully with clients and stakeholders. You’ll bring a genuine curiosity for the evolving spatial industry and the judgement to apply your skills thoughtfully as technologies and client requirements continue to shift.

This role can be based in either Brisbane, Gold Coast, Sunshine Coast or Toowoomba. 

Working with an energetic and high-performing team, you will: 

Develop and deliver geospatial and data science products and services, ensuring compliance with quality assurance standards. 
Engage proactively with clients, with support from the LI Team Lead, to identify insights and opportunities contributing to the team’s pipeline targets and future business development. 
Collaborate with technical leads across the business to support GHD’s business goals and KPIs. 
Contribute to the growth and successful performance of LI capabilities, including workflow design and automation, solution implementation, and administration of spatial databases and web mapping and dashboarding environments. 
Lead proposal preparation, including input collation, writing, and technical documentation. 

What you will bring to the team: 

Bachelor's degree in Spatial Science, or equivalent with a minimum 7 years of professional experience in similar industries.
Highly developed oral, written and visual communication skills, with an emphasis on being able to convey ideas and outcomes to a broad range of stakeholders.
Strong spatial analysis skills with the ability to work across software solutions
Data management skills, including an understanding of geospatial data models, schemas and standards.
Data collection form design and development with an eye to optimising for user experience and automation
Demonstrated experience in the use of AI technology in an industry use case
The ability to design and develop automated workflow solutions
Proficiency in ESRI GIS Software Suite
Experience in Technical Project Management, Client Engagement, and Relationship Management. 

Preferable experience in: 

GeoAI workflows and solutions
GIS Development Skills
FME
Technical Business Analysis
PowerBI
Data Modelling

ROLE SUMMARY

In this role, you will act as the critical link between day‑to‑day finance and operational activities and CRE's broader digital transformation agenda. With a strong grounding in ERP systems, data models and business process mapping, you will partner with finance, operations and technology stakeholders to improve business processes, system integrations, reporting capability and provide insights.

You will work across a modern, multi‑system environment to design, enhance and maintain automated, system‑led processes for financial reporting, analysis, budgeting and forecasting. This is a hands‑on, delivery‑focused role requiring strong analytical capability, commercial thinking and the ability to translate technical solutions into value-add business outcomes.

ROLE RESPONSIBILITIES

Systems, Data & Integrations

-       Maintain system integrations

-       Manage system security/access

-       Design data models

-       Ensure data accuracy

Reporting, Analytics & Insights

-       Build BI dashboards

-       Automate reporting processes

-       Define analytics data frameworks

-       Generate value-add insights

Stakeholder Engagement

-       Partner with business stakeholders

-       Train and support users

-       Liaise with system consultants

Systems & Process Improvement

-       Document end-to-end processes

-       Resolve system data issues

-       Gather user systems feedback

-       Continuous process improvement

-       Continuous systems improvement

YOUR CAPABILITY

You are commercially minded, analytically strong and naturally curious about how technology can improve business performance. You are comfortable working across both finance and systems domains and thrive in environments where you can translate technical detail into clear business impact.

You bring a proactive mindset, strong work ethic and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment. Collaborative and approachable, you engage confidently with stakeholders across the business, anticipate issues before they arise and propose practical, system‑led solutions. You take pride in delivering high‑quality outcomes, pay close attention to detail and enjoy contributing to initiatives that drive lasting improvement.

Professional Experience 

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Information Systems, Commerce or a related discipline.

3–6 years’ experience in a finance analyst or systems analyst role within a multi‑system environment.

Hands‑on experience with ERP systems and data platforms, with exposure to Sage Intacct and Snowflake highly regarded.

Demonstrated experience building and maintaining Power BI models or other business intelligence & data visualisation tools.

Experience using scripting or automation tools to improve reporting or finance processes.

Strong understanding of relational databases, data modelling and process flow design.

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills and strong familiarity with Microsoft 365 tools including PowerPoint, SharePoint, Visio and Excel.

Strong communication, time‑management and project delivery capability, with the ability to work both independently and as part of a team.

COMPANY SUMMARY

CRE Group is a leading civil construction and material processing contractor operating across Victoria, New South Wales, South Australia, and Queensland. Our company continues to grow rapidly, with projects diversified across civil construction, bulk earthworks, remediation works, landfill construction, and crushing & screening services.

We are committed to delivering excellence in our industry, driven by our core values of culture, respect, and excellence. Our people are collaborative, hardworking and supportive. Their commitment to the company’s success fuels our growth, and in turn, creates opportunities within CRE Group for our people.

Our federal government and private sector clients are looking for TSPV Data Specialists to deliver and maintain Australia's strategic advantage and capability edge over the coming decade. Your work will ensure that our nation remains one step ahead, equipped with the insights necessary to safeguard our interests at home and abroad.

Working on top-secret projects and having access to cutting-edge technology are among the exciting prospects that contractors can look forward to. As is the security of long-term work in programs with a wide range of job opportunities for individuals with various skills and expertise. These roles are based in Canberra, Melbourne, Sydney and Brisbane and requires an Australian Top Secret Positive Vetting (TSPV) security clearance. These opportunities do not have the ability to obtain/upgrade sponsorship for this security clearance.

These are initial 12-month contracts with extension options.

Estimated start, late 2026.

Whether you're ready to take the next step in your career or simply exploring new opportunities, we encourage you to apply and or reach out to myself directly for a confidential discussion.

We are looking for all backgrounds and levels of experience - Junior, Mid, and Senior!

We are looking to speak to Data Specialists in the below skillsets:

Database Administrators
Data Engineers
Data Scientists
Data Managers
Data Analysts
Data Architect

Why Kinexus?

Kinexus has been supplying contractors to Federal Government panels since 2004 and have a strong relationship with them.
Our experience in managing contractors in defence/national security industries means we understand the requirements from the Commonwealth, insurances and clearances.
DISP accredited; we can hold clearances and manage renewals.

Head of Research, Property

This is not a typical research role.

At Blue Wealth Property, research is the foundation of everything we do. It underpins every recommendation, every property we select, and the trust we build with our clients and business partners.

We are now looking for a Head of Research to lead this function, elevate it further, and ensure it continues to set the standard in the market.

This is a career-defining role for the right person.

Why Blue Wealth Property

At Blue Wealth Property, you’ll be joining one of Australia’s most trusted property research and advisory groups. With over 17 years in the market, our reputation has been built on the strength, integrity and consistency of our research.

Our methodology is independently audited – something that sets us apart, and reflects our commitment to delivering disciplined, research-backed property decisions. Research is not a support function here; it is at the core of our business.

You’ll be working alongside a high-performing, collaborative team in a business where research is valued, respected, and central to every outcome. This is an environment where your thinking will be heard, your expertise will be trusted, and your work will directly influence the success of our clients, partners, and the business.

The Role

You will take ownership of the research function across the business, leading the team, refining our methodology, and ensuring research is consistently driving confident, high-quality decisions.

Your role will span leadership, strategy, and execution.

You will:

Lead and develop the research team to deliver high-quality, timely support across the business

Provide clear, research-backed justification to support Sales, BDMs, Client Services, and our Business Partners

Own and evolve our proprietary research methodology to ensure it remains best in market

Influence which properties we select - and which we don’t - based on strong research conviction

Produce high-quality research content including annual reports, event material, blogs, and client-facing insights

Present at events and become a trusted voice of research within the business and to our partners

Educate and uplift the broader team, building confidence and consistency in how research is understood and applied

Monitor and report on the performance of property recommendations over time

About You

You are a commercial research leader who understands how insight drives outcomes.

You bring:

Experience in property research

Strong commercial instinct - you understand how research translates into decisions and results

The ability to simplify complex data into clear, compelling narratives

Confidence to present, influence, and challenge thinking

Leadership capability - you can build, guide and elevate a team

High standards, ownership, and accountability

You are decisive, pragmatic, and solutions focused. You bring energy, presence, and conviction to your role.

If you are looking for a role where your thinking drives real outcomes - and where you can build something meaningful, this is it.

$140,000 - $170,000 + Superannuation + Salary Packaging ($15,900.00 + Meal & Entertainment expenses)

Located on the lands of the Bidiagal Clan of the Dharawal Nation (Randwick, NSW), Black Dog Institute is the only Medical Research Institute in Australia to investigate mental health across the lifespan, our aim is to create a mentally healthier world for everyone. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.  

The Senior Digital Insights & Reporting Analyst plays a key role in supporting Bank Australia’s Digital & Experience function by delivering high‑quality digital analytics, reporting and performance insights across the CMO division. The role transforms data from multiple sources into actionable intelligence that enables customer‑centred, data‑driven decision‑making across Digital, Brand and Marketing teams. Through automated reporting solutions and proactive performance monitoring, the role identifies opportunities to optimise digital channels, enhance customer engagement and support the bank’s growth objectives, while ensuring continuous improvement and compliance across customer‑facing digital platforms.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s most trusted bank and a leading purpose-driven business.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Deliver accurate, timely reporting and dashboards across digital channels (website, app, digital banking and acquisition funnels) to provide visibility of performance and customer behaviour.
Lead CRM performance reporting across email, SMS, app and internet banking, analysing lifecycle programs to improve engagement, conversion and retention.
Analyse digital journeys, campaigns and customer behaviour to identify trends, opportunities and areas for optimisation.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights and executive‑ready narratives that support digital experience and growth objectives.
Build and enhance automated reporting solutions using tools such as Power BI, Google tools and SQL, reducing manual effort and improving efficiency.
Support consistent integration, tracking and governance of digital data sources, ensuring high data quality and reliable insights.
Proactively monitor digital performance to identify anomalies, friction points or emerging issues, supporting root‑cause analysis and resolution.
Ensure analytics and reporting comply with privacy, regulatory and data‑governance standards, maintaining clear documentation and audit support.
Partner closely with internal teams and external analytics and development partners to deliver ad‑hoc insights, deep dives and ongoing performance reporting.

What you will bring

Degree in data analytics, information systems, marketing analytics or related discipline.
Strong experience in digital analytics, BI or reporting roles.
Experience supporting digital channels (websites, mobile apps or online banking), with knowledge of tagging, CX or marketing analytics desirable.
Experience in regulated environments (e.g. banking or financial services) highly regarded.
High attention to detail and commitment to data integrity, privacy and quality standards.
Demonstrated experience building automated, interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI, Looker or similar BI tools.
Practical experience using digital analytics platforms (e.g. Google Analytics or Adobe Analytics) to interpret behavioural and performance data.
Strong data capability including advanced Excel, SQL, and exposure to data modelling, DAX and reporting automation tools.
Proven ability to translate complex digital data into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical audiences.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a collaborative approach and ability to deliver to deadlines in a fast‑paced environment.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

Manager, Data Platform Engineering

The Data Platform Engineering Manager leads the team responsible for designing, developing, and delivering enterprise data platforms and reusable data products. This role is critical to ensuring scalable, secure, and high-performing data capabilities that power insights, decision-making, automation, and future AI use cases.

You will oversee the end-to-end development lifecycle, from backlog planning through to tested and automated deployment, while ensuring quality, reusability, and platform alignment. This role also plays a key leadership function in working across teams to shape solutions that enable strategic outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the engineering team responsible for building data pipelines, models, and reusable components using Snowflake and AWS-native tools.

Translate business and analytical requirements into well-designed data products, aligned to domain needs and platform strategy.

Oversee the design and delivery of scalable data models, semantic layers, and data marts.

Embed strong solution design practices and partner with architects to ensure alignment with enterprise patterns.

Manage and coach a multidisciplinary team including Data Engineers, Solution Designers, DevOps Engineers, and Data Test Engineers.

Ensure development standards, best practices, and governance are consistently applied.

Drive adoption of test-driven development, CI/CD, and automation practices across the team.

Collaborate with Data Ops to ensure smooth deployment, observability, and post-release support.

Continuously improve engineering maturity, velocity, and reusability of data products across service lines.

Contribute to GenAI and Agentic AI readiness by building modern, AI-enabled data foundations.

Inspire and lead a high-performing engineering team, fostering a culture of accountability, technical excellence, and continuous learning.

Support Agile ways of working and contribute to the design of an evolving, service-aligned operating model.

Essential Criteria

7+ years in Data Engineering, including 3+ years in a leadership or senior technical role.

Proven experience leading Data Engineering or Data Platform teams in complex, cloud-native environments.

Strong technical knowledge of modern data platforms, including Snowflake and AWS.

Strong SQL capability and deep understanding of data pipeline design, ELT/ETL patterns, and data modelling principles.

Experience across data architecture, data solution design, scalable data models and reusable data products.

Demonstrated success delivering scalable data pipelines and reusable data products.

Experience implementing engineering standards, code reviews, and reusable frameworks.

Ability to lead multidisciplinary technical teams across development lifecycles.

Practice management experience, including setting standards, improving engineering maturity, and overseeing delivery across parallel projects.

Proficiency in CI/CD, testing frameworks, and deployment automation.

Excellent stakeholder communication and collaboration skills.

Nice to Haves

Informatica.

SAS ETL.

dbt.

Certifications in AWS, Snowflake or similar.

Experience in Agile delivery environments, virtual squads, or service-aligned team models.

Exposure to real-time data ingestion and event-driven architecture.

Familiarity with data mesh or federated data product design.

Knowledge of data governance, metadata, and lineage practices.

Understanding of public sector or regulated industry environments.

At Rabobank, our commitment to our employees and clients is at the heart of everything we do.

As the world’s leading specialist in food and agribusiness banking, one of our key strengths lies in our people who have a deep understanding of agriculture and are committed to adding long-term value for clients.

Our culture is unique, because every day our people work knowing that we are all playing our part in supporting our farmers to feed the world. We strive to create an open, balanced, and flexible workplace where people feel that they belong, can be open and honest, and are supported. We actively encourage collaboration, knowledge sharing, and continuous development, and we are focused on growing each other.

About the role:

RaboResearch is Rabobank’s global knowledge and strategic insights provider. Our global and regional network of analysts are trusted sources of insights for clients, colleagues, policymakers, the industry, and the media -known for independent insights, rigorous analysis, and clear communication on the forces shaping food and agriculture.

The RaboResearch Australia & New Zealand team is looking for a Commodity Analyst to cover some of today’s most dynamic and fast‑moving parts of the agricultural value chain. This role is designed for someone who enjoys learning, collaborating, and contributing ideas in a supportive, high‑impact environment. This is an opportunity to make a difference using your analytical and communication skills and your interest in the food and agribusiness sector.

Based preferably in Sydney (Melbourne or Brisbane might be considered), this role focuses on farm inputs, including fertilisers, agrochemicals, farmland values, and farm machinery—as well as fibres, with a particular emphasis on cotton and wool. These sectors sit at the centre of major structural change, driving price volatility and technology adoption (including AI), while being heavily influenced by geopolitics, climate risk, and evolving global demand.

This is a crucial and influential role supporting the bank and its rural and corporate clients in their short-and long-term strategic decision making. We are looking for a candidate who enjoys combining data, economics, and industry knowledge—and who is equally comfortable writing, presenting, and speaking publicly.

The Analyst will be required to:

Deliver thought provoking, high‑quality market and industry research on (fertiliser/farmland prices/agrochemicals) and fibres (cotton/wool) for internal stakeholders and external clients across Australia and New Zealand

Analyse key drivers such as price volatility, cost structures, technology adoption, regulation, sustainability, and global trade flows

Contribute actively to RaboResearch’s global coverage team, partnering closely with colleagues across the world to produce consistently strong global insights and utilise those locally.

Produce engaging research outputs, including thought‑leadership reports, podcasts, presentations, videos, and digital content, with support from colleagues and established processes.

Act as the sector expert, building credibility and confidence when presenting insights at client events, industry conferences, field days, and internal strategy sessions across Australia and New Zealand

Build a public profile as a trusted voice at a pace that suits your experience and career stage, engaging with print, radio, TV, and digital media on key market and industry developments.

Work closely with Rabobank’s rural and corporate banking teams to support client conversations, identify commercial opportunities, and inform strategic thinking.

Provide research input into credit policy, sector strategy, and risk assessment, including presentations to senior management when required.

You will be encouraged to take ownership of your research while being well supported by a collaborative, trans‑Tasman and global team that covers food and agri research across crops, livestock, and consumer foods. You will benefit from joining a diverse, respectful, and inclusive team of analysts.

To be successful in the role, you will have: 

Bachelor’s Degree in related field required.

Deep industry knowledge, data literacy and technical skills, storytelling and communication and commercial mindset and ability to work across functions/geographies.

A strong analytical mindset and genuine curiosity about how agricultural markets work

Solid understanding of agriculture, agribusiness, or commodity markets gained through professional, academic, or lived experience—ideally with exposure to farm inputs (fertiliser/farmland prices/agrochemicals) and/or fibres (cotton and wool)

Confidence working with large data sets, building models, and interpreting quantitative and qualitative information, with the willingness to keep learning and ask questions.

The ability to translate complex analysis and results in clear messages and a memorable story that engage a wide range of audiences - from conferences and board rooms to kitchen tables on farm.

Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability (or ambition) to present to diverse audiences and engage with media.

A client-focused and commercial mindset and the ability to take the perspective of the client and analyze the forces that shape the future of their business.

High proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint, and comfort using new technologies and data tools to enhance analysis.

A strong collaborative mindset and willingness to connect the dots by leveraging our global and local network.

Flexibility to travel regularly and represent RaboResearch at client and industry events.

A desire to keep learning and to use technology—including emerging analytical tools—as a competitive advantage.

A positive, open mindset and the confidence to challenge established thinking in a constructive and respectful way, and to deliver clear forward-looking messages and scenarios in times of high uncertainty.

This role is well‑suited for experienced commodity analysts. Equally, this could also be an opportunity for early‑career agricultural economists and data‑driven graduates with a farming background who are eager to deepen their sector knowledge and build a public profile.

If you are early in your career, you will benefit from:

Working alongside experienced RaboResearch analysts in Australia and across our global network

Hands‑on exposure to real‑world market analysis that directly informs client decisions.

Opportunities to develop your writing, presentation, and media engagement skills.

Ongoing coaching, mentorship, feedback and support from local and global peers

We value potential, curiosity, analytical thinking, and the right attitude as much as prior experience. If you’re excited by this role and you are motivation to keep learning, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet every requirement listed.

What we're proud to offer you: 

Rabo@Anywhere is our hybrid way of working at Rabobank and for Australia, that means we have a blend of time working between the office and home.  

Wellbeing leave. These leave days support greater work/life balance and is just another way we are helping our employees to lead happier, healthier, and more fulfilling lives. 

Education Assistance Program. Rabobank values the development of its people and has a great Education Assistance Program to assist with professional development. 

Parental Leave that supports you and your family while giving you the freedom and flexibility to enjoy this special time (up-to 14 weeks paid leave) 

Bonus and Additional Leave. We realise sometimes 4 weeks Annual Leave isn't enough! At Rabobank we provide an incentive for eligible employees to receive 1 extra week Annual Leave and an option to purchase another 2 weeks Annual Leave. 

Extra Insurance. Rabobank recognises that employees need to protect their financial wellbeing, in the event of serious illness, injury, or even death. Rabobank provides eligible permanent employees with employer-funded Death, TPD and Income Protection Insurance. 

Rabo Workplace Giving program. As part of Rabobank's global Corporate Social Responsibility, Workplace Giving aims to encourage employees to contribute to our community. Rabo Workplace Giving program matches employee donations to selected Social Partners.

Our Values: 

Rabobank Australia values inclusion, belonging, and positive experiences for all. Our work environment, our benefits, and the way we live our values, “Client Driven”, “Responsible”, “Professional” and “Cooperative” make it a great place to work. We welcome applicants from diverse backgrounds. 

Please let our Talent Acquisition team know if you need any accommodations to make our opportunities more accessible to you. 

Rabobank is not accepting submissions from recruitment agencies for this role. 

Applicants must hold valid work rights for this role.

Job Title: Associate Professor or Professor of Marketing, Technology and Innovation
Ad Text:
Full-Time, Continuing position
Melbourne (Bundoora) location
Level D Classification: Base salary from $172,864 + 17% superannuation
Level E Classification: Base salary of $221,486 + 17% superannuation
Hybrid work model supported

About the position

The Associate Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation provides leadership to advance interdisciplinary research and teaching in AI-driven marketing and digital innovation. This position fosters collaboration across disciplines to strengthen the curriculum, ensuring it remains contemporary and relevant to industry trends. The role also supports scholarship that addresses the fast-changing landscape of marketing through AI, data, platforms, and new technologies, with a focus on ethical and responsible use of AI.

The Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation is responsible for advancing La Trobe Business School’s strategic direction in marketing, with a focus on artificial intelligence, digital innovation, and analytics. This position drives curriculum development, high-impact research, and industry engagement, while fostering interdisciplinary collaboration to position the School as a leader in future-focused, industry-relevant education. The Professor is nationally and internationally recognized for contributions to AI-enabled marketing and customer analytics, and is instrumental in preparing graduates for success in evolving, technology-driven environments.

Skills and Experience

The Associate Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation must hold a PhD in Marketing or a related discipline, demonstrate effective academic leadership, and expertise in marketing, digital innovation, and AI-driven practices. The position requires a strong research record, cross-disciplinary collaboration, curriculum innovation, digital learning proficiency, ability to build partnerships and secure funding, postgraduate supervision, mentoring experience, and excellent communication skills for collaboration across academic and professional groups.

The Professor, Marketing, Technology and Innovation must have a PhD or equivalent in Marketing or a related field, demonstrate effective senior academic leadership, recognized expertise in marketing, digital innovation, and AI, and a strong record of cross-disciplinary collaboration. Essential experience includes a distinguished research profile with national or international recognition, leadership in teaching and curriculum innovation, mentoring experience, and excellent communication skills for collaboration across academic and professional groups.

Please refer to the Position Descriptions for other duties, skills and experience required for this position.

Ad Text:
About Us 

Established in 1946, Teys Australia is a beef processing and food production business. We are best known for providing quality Aussie beef to customers here in Australia and around the world. 

With operations across 14 locations in Qld, NSW, Victoria, and South Australia, we are the second largest beef processor and exporter in Australia, employing over 5,000 passionate people who drive our success, and bring their very best to work every day. 

Together, we focus on feeding people and enriching lives. Join us! 

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced and forward-thinking Rewards & People Insights Managerto help to elevate our remuneration governance and workforce reporting capability across the Teys Group.

Reporting to te Enterprise General Manager People & Culture, this role plays a pivotal part in remuneration governance, ensuring market competitiveness and driving evidence‑based workforce analytics that support informed, enterprise‑wide decision making.

This is a unique opportunity for a commercially savvy remuneration and dataspecialist who thrives working in cross-functional teams and excels in a collaborative environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead remuneration governance, job evaluation, benchmarking and annual review cycles. 

Delver high‑quality workforce analytics, dashboards and predictive insights. 

Partner with HR, Finance and Operations to support workforce modelling on costs and inform decisions.
Provide clear reward advice, reporting and data integrity oversight across the enterprise.


About You

Essential:

3–5+ years’ experience in remuneration, reward, workforce analytics or commercial HR. 
Strong analytical capability with advanced Excel and data modelling skills (Tableau experience highly regarded).
Experienceusing Chris21(payroll), UKG, and Tableau reports

Experience with Korn Ferry (Hay Group) evaluations and salary benchmarking. 

Confidence navigating enterprise agreements, awards or industrial frameworks. 

Experience in blue‑collar, manufacturing or FMCG business highly regarded. 

Commercial acumen, strong judgement and absolute discretion with sensitive data.


What We Offer

At Teys, we believe great leadership should be supported by meaningful rewards and a workplace where you can thrive. As part of our team, you will enjoy:

Competitive remuneration package + performance incentives
Unique annual meat allowance 

Salary packaging options including devices and novated leasing 

Health, wellbeing and continuity support 

Injury/illness salary continuance scheme

Access to corporate discounts including Bupa healthcare 

Genuine investment in your development and a clear pathway to future growth

To Apply

If you are looking for an opportunity to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment within a growing business offering excellent career development and opportunities, we encourage you to APPLY today.  

For a confidential discussion about this role, Luisa Hamze on 07 •••• •196.

You can check us out at www.teysgroup.com.au or join us on https://www.facebook.com/TeysAustralia.

Not all heroes wear capes — but plenty of them use toilet paper

Want to challenge yourself at a purpose-led scale-up AND make a difference in the world? Come join the revolootion! We’re a leading eco-friendly household essentials business, and we’re a bit different: we donate 50% of our profits to help everyone gain access to clean water and a toilet. As of June 2024, we’ve contributed over $18 million AUD to this mission.

Basically, we’re really good at making it fun to do good. We operate scaling businesses in the UK, US, Canada and Australia, supported by an amazing team of 250+ people globally. Over the next 5-10 years, we’re aiming to increase our annual donation tenfold. That’s where you come in.

A bit about the role


As our Director of Pricing & Revenue Management, you are the master architect of our global value exchange. You’ll turn complex data into clear pricing and promotional strategy that ensures our rolls keep rolling into homes everywhere while hitting our ambitious commercial goals.

Operating as a leader within our Commercial Strategy and Insight team, you’ll be a powerhouse of data and insight-led thinking. You won't just be crunching numbers; you’ll be solving complex problems, developing clear narratives and driving strategic action across regional and global teams to ensure our growth is both rapid and sustainable.

If you worked here this past month, here are some things you might have been involved in:

Leading Price Transformations: Analysing market dynamics, scenario-modelling, recommending and then executing omni-channel pricing change, managing resources and collaborating cross-functionally to ensure seamless delivery. 
Shaping Long-term Strategy: Developing long-term pricing principles to feed into the 5 year strategy.
Promotional Analysis: Conducting a deep-dive on our last 6 promotions, delivering clear insights on what worked and what didn’t work, and translating findings into concrete recommendations and guardrails for future campaigns.
Testing & Learning: Ideating new ways to expand our promotional programme, and launching a promotional test-and-learn plan to validate hypotheses and find new ways to hit our commercial goals.
Retail Expansion: Partnering with regional retail teams and peer Commercial Strategy directors to prepare SKU and range proposals for new retailer entrances, building detailed cost and scenario models, finding the sweet spot for the shopper, the retailer and Who Gives A Crap, and translating that analysis into a clear range recommendation for the Executive team.

Let’s talk about you

You're a Commercial Visionary
You have a talent for building thoughtful models and enjoy exploring the "why" behind the data. But you don't stop there — you have a knack for translating that analysis into simple, persuasive narratives, and commercial strategies that stick. You're comfortable challenging the status quo and navigating difficult conversations with radical candor when the data suggests a more effective path forward.

You’re a Technical Powerhouse
Whether analysing past performance or modelling future scenarios, you thrive in the detail and have high-level proficiency with business analytics tools. You’re an expert in financial modelling, encompassing incrementality, ROI, manufacturing and logistics costs, price elasticity, unit economics and volumetrics. You can juggle multiple high-stakes projects across regions without losing sight of the long-term strategic vision.

You’re a collaborative problem solver
You value building strong connections across the business, acting as a trusted advisor and a supportive sounding board for leaders to help them navigate complex questions.

You have a wide lens
You understand the broad picture of an omni-channel, multi-market organisation and can optimise pricing and price ladders across different channels, in accordance with the channel roles, so that they make sense for consumers whilst hitting commercial goals.  

You give a crap

Do you want your work to make the world a better place? Do you want to work with other people who feel the same way? What a coincidence — us too!

Why should you work with us?

First off, as a certified B Corp™, we work to make a difference every day while maintaining the highest standards of social and environmental impact. With our most recent score of 125.5, we’re in very good company with other exceptional B Corps around the world.

We also believe that helping to make the world a better place should be rewarded accordingly. That’s why we offer:

Competitive, market-informed salaries.
Meaningful healthcare support for our global team.
Generous paid leave and tailored learning and development opportunities.
Free toilet paper (yes, you read that right).

Our team is really proud (95%) to work for Who Gives A Crap, and we couldn’t be more proud of that.

Have you made it this far?

If you’re still reading, we think there’s a strong chance you might be our kind of person.

Here’s the thing, though — research suggests that 60% of women and underrepresented folks might have already talked themselves out of applying. Even if you don’t check every box above, we want to encourage you to introduce yourself. We believe a diversity of perspectives makes a team stronger — and the stronger our team, the closer we are to delivering toilets and clean water for all.

About you

Has significant experience in business analysis within complex projects
Has experience with MES, QMS or enterprise system implementations
Can analyse and translate complex business requirements into solutions
Has strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Has experience working across transformation or digital projects
Has knowledge of healthcare, manufacturing or GMP environments (desirable)

 

Why work with us?

Work alongside passionate professionals in a supportive and inclusive environment that values people and prioritises employee success and wellbeing. This includes

Competitive salaries

12.75% superannuation
17.5% leave loading

Employee wellbeing

36.25 hour full time work week
Access to 24/7 confidential employee support providers and counsellors including for immediate family members
Additional flexibility to support your work life balance including access to generous leave entitlements, such as purchased leave, domestic violence leave, reproductive health leave, cultural leave, study and examination leave.

Professional development

Career progression with more than 300 diverse career pathways and career support to take you further!
Access to a variety of programs and initiatives to support training and career growth.
